operational_knowledge_units.title: Estop model stores estop identity, Geek area ID, and active estop status
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide states that callbacks are received from Geek for estops, and that the Estop model shows the estop name, Geek AreaId, and whether it is actively estopped.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'We receive callbacks from Geek for estops.'
Slide text: 'Estop model shows the Name of the estop, Geeks AreaId, and if its actively estopped.'

relationships.evidence: We receive callbacks from Geek for estops.

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Sorter Sweeper Database Estop model reference. Geek sends callbacks for estops. The Estop model contains the estop name, Geek AreaId, and whether the estop is actively estopped.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Estop release creates an estop activity record
operational_knowledge_units.summary: When an estop is released, the system adds a record to the estop activity model.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'When an Estop is released a record is added to estop activity model.'

relationships.evidence: When an Estop is released a record is added to estop activity model.

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Estop release behavior in Sorter Sweeper Database. When an estop is released, a record is added to the estop activity model.

discarded_candidates.title: Handle estop callback or review estop data
discarded_candidates.reason: The slide describes database fields and event-driven recording behavior but does not provide an explicit operator or support action sequence, so no reusable procedure can be extracted without inventing steps.
